mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in unemployment rate above 5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP and industrial production indicates strong economic momentum, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days through improved corporate earnings expectations.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods of simultaneous GDP and INDPRO growth are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Monetary tightening cycles, indicated by rising federal funds rates above 4.5%, compress equity valuations through higher discount rates, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite rising FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent disinflation, measured by declining CPI, signals accommodative monetary policy, boosting S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days through reduced real interest rates.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in disinflation periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts below 1,000,000 units signals weak demand for construction, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days through sector-specific earnings declines.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite falling HOUST.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth combined with low unemployment indicates tight labor markets, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through elevated corporate profit margins.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods of high PAYEMS and low UNRATE are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sudden drop in industrial production below its 12-month moving average signals supply chain disruptions, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days through sector-specific volatility.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite falling INDPRO.
